title: Streaming Analysis, Chunk Boundaries, and Resource Budgets · Python Build Room
summary: A library-tools pair incrementally builds bounded streaming analyzer from an original fixed text fixture while retaining earlier Git, Python, Unicode, determinism, bounded-resource, accessibility, testing, and evidence requirements. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of bounded streaming analyzer while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Process lines or bounded chunks incrementally and keep only state required by the declared report.
Carry a bounded token fragment across chunk boundaries and prove concatenated-chunk equivalence.
Use an incremental decoder or text stream that preserves multibyte sequences and explicit error policy.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-bookbot-python cumulative Unicode-safe text-analysis portfolio.

Browser execution uses original fixed in-memory fixtures and deterministic pure-Python models only.
Host files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, package installation, native profiling, load,
operating-system races, publication, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class TextPolicy:
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    normalization: str = "NFC"
    casefold: bool = True
    top_k: int = 10


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    corpus_id: str
    revision: str
    policy_version: str
    command_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Bookbot portfolio ready"

steps.title: Bookbot and Python Commit to the mission model · Workshop Stream Iteration
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop Bookbot team handles case 1087 during an assistive-technology terminal review. Build deterministic Python evidence to prove streaming and batch equivalence under token-fragment and peak-memory bounds; record the first failed boundary, causal evidence, repair, regression test, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner; then install the built wheel in a fresh environment and rerun success, invalid-argument, and decode-failure cases. Browser code uses pure bounded functions and original fixed fixtures only. Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. steps.title: Bookbot and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Batch Stream Equivalence
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: bookbot-batch-stream-equivalence-1a2bbs" that uses case 3444 to prove streaming and batch equivalence under token-fragment and peak-memory bounds. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not open host files, run processes or Git, install packages, read credentials or host state, publish artifacts, or cause external effects; verify native boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, Unicode 17, disposable files and environments, real CLI and package runs, profiling, accessibility, security, load, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

Evidence target: Bookbot and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Batch Stream Equivalence.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Batch Stream Equivalence into observable performance evidence. For Bookbot and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Batch Stream Equivalence, keep Batch Stream Equivalence connected to executable Python text-analysis, command, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: bookbot-batch-stream-equivalence-1a2bbs
# Competency: bookbot-batch-stream-equivalence.
# Case 3444: a digital-humanities corpus revision.
# Operating constraint: keep stdout machine-safe, diagnostics on stderr, status explicit, and every signal understandable without color.
# Changed case: split one word and one UTF-8 sequence across stream chunks and compare batch output byte-for-byte.
def evidence_bookbot_batch_stream_equivalence_1a2bbs(chunks: int = 8, batch_tokens: int = 120, stream_tokens: int = 120, peak_bytes: int = 8192, memory_budget: int = 16384, fragments_closed: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1a2bbs = "1a2bbs-sbb2a1-3444"
    assert evidence_variant_1a2bbs
    evidence_axes_1a2bbs = (
        "corpus1a2bbs",
        "policysbb2a1",
        "revision3444",
        "command1a2bbs3444",
        "reportsbb2a13444",
        "test34441a2bbs",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1a2bbs)) == 6
    if chunks < 1 or batch_tokens < 0 or stream_tokens < 0:
        return False, stream_tokens, "stream-shape"
    if stream_tokens != batch_tokens:
        return False, stream_tokens, "batch-stream-drift"
    if peak_bytes < 0 or peak_bytes > memory_budget:
        return False, peak_bytes, "memory-budget"
    if not fragments_closed:
        return False, chunks, "partial-token"
    assert peak_bytes <= memory_budget
    return True, stream_tokens, "bounded-stream"

Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. steps.title: Bookbot and Python Test an acceptance boundary · Workshop Batch Stream Equivalence
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop Bookbot team handles case 2756 during a large-file migration rehearsal. Build deterministic Python evidence to prove streaming and batch equivalence under token-fragment and peak-memory bounds; preserve original text and offsets beside every normalized comparison key and report exclusion; then change LF to CRLF, remove the final newline, and preserve the declared word and line policy. Browser code uses pure bounded functions and original fixed fixtures only. Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Compare batch and streaming results across adversarial split points, malformed data, and final fragments.
